text.content: 1.1  Row Expression Recognition  [s1_1_row_expression_recognition]

rich_text.plain_text: 1.1  Row Expression Recognition  [s1_1_row_expression_recognition]

text.content: A 2×7 dot array appears centrally on screen. No highlighting.

rich_text.plain_text: A 2×7 dot array appears centrally on screen. No highlighting.

text.content: "Here's an array. You got really good at reading these last time. Either by rows or columns, two different expressions. But this array is hiding something in its numbers. Let's see if you can spot it."

rich_text.plain_text: "Here's an array. You got really good at reading these last time. Either by rows or columns, two different expressions. But this array is hiding something in its numbers. Let's see if you can spot it."

text.content: Horizontal lines highlighted showing 2 rows of 7 dots each.

rich_text.plain_text: Horizontal lines highlighted showing 2 rows of 7 dots each.

text.content: "I'm highlighting the rows. See them going across? There are 2 rows, and each row has 7 dots. Which expression matches what you see?"

rich_text.plain_text: "I'm highlighting the rows. See them going across? There are 2 rows, and each row has 7 dots. Which expression matches what you see?"

text.content:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["2 × 7", "7 × 2", "7 + 2", "2 + 7"]
"Which expression describes the rows?"

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["2 × 7", "7 × 2", "7 + 2", "2 + 7"]
"Which expression describes the rows?"

text.content: ✅ Student selected 2 × 7, correct

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected 2 × 7, correct

text.content: "Right. 2 times 7. 2 rows of 7."

rich_text.plain_text: "Right. 2 times 7. 2 rows of 7."

text.content: ❌ Student selected 7 × 2, reversed the factors

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 7 × 2, reversed the factors

text.content: Row highlights pulse to emphasize the 2 rows

rich_text.plain_text: Row highlights pulse to emphasize the 2 rows

text.content: "You're close. We want the expression based on the rows so the first number tells us how many rows. Count the rows going across. How many rows do you see?"

rich_text.plain_text: "You're close. We want the expression based on the rows so the first number tells us how many rows. Count the rows going across. How many rows do you see?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected 7 + 2, used addition instead of multiplication

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 7 + 2, used addition instead of multiplication

text.content: Row highlights remain visible

rich_text.plain_text: Row highlights remain visible

text.content: "That's not it. The array has equal groups. We use multiplication for rows of equal groups, not addition. The rows show 2 groups of 7. How do you write that as a multiplication expression?"

rich_text.plain_text: "That's not it. The array has equal groups. We use multiplication for rows of equal groups, not addition. The rows show 2 groups of 7. How do you write that as a multiplication expression?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected 2 + 7, used addition instead of multiplication

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 2 + 7, used addition instead of multiplication

text.content: Row highlights pulse to draw attention to the repeated groups

rich_text.plain_text: Row highlights pulse to draw attention to the repeated groups

text.content: "Addition combines amounts to find a total. There are not 9 total dots in this array. Here we have 2 equal rows. When we have equal groups, we multiply. Match this array to a multiplication expression."

rich_text.plain_text: "Addition combines amounts to find a total. There are not 9 total dots in this array. Here we have 2 equal rows. When we have equal groups, we multiply. Match this array to a multiplication expression."

text.content: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

text.content: First row pulses, then second row pulses. Text '2 rows' appears. Then '7 in each row' appears below.

rich_text.plain_text: First row pulses, then second row pulses. Text '2 rows' appears. Then '7 in each row' appears below.

text.content: "Let me show you how to think through this. I look at the rows going across and count them. 1, 2. That's 2 rows. Each row has 7 dots. That's the row description, 2 groups of 7. Equal groups are multiplication. So we write 2 times 7. "

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you how to think through this. I look at the rows going across and count them. 1, 2. That's 2 rows. Each row has 7 dots. That's the row description, 2 groups of 7. Equal groups are multiplication. So we write 2 times 7. "

text.content: 2.1  Product Observation Notice Wonder  [s2_1_product_observation_notice_wonder]

rich_text.plain_text: 2.1  Product Observation Notice Wonder  [s2_1_product_observation_notice_wonder]

text.content: 2×7 array appears, showing row organization. 2 rows, 7 items per row.

rich_text.plain_text: 2×7 array appears, showing row organization. 2 rows, 7 items per row.

text.content: Row expression appears: 2 × 7 = 14

rich_text.plain_text: Row expression appears: 2 × 7 = 14

text.content: Array transitions to show column emphasis. 7 columns, 2 items per column.

rich_text.plain_text: Array transitions to show column emphasis. 7 columns, 2 items per column.

text.content: Column expression appears: 7 × 2 = 14

rich_text.plain_text: Column expression appears: 7 × 2 = 14

text.content: "Now check this out. Here's the row expression AND the column expression, both with their products. Look at those two products. What do you notice?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Now check this out. Here's the row expression AND the column expression, both with their products. Look at those two products. What do you notice?"

text.content: tool: multiple_choice  options:

rich_text.plain_text: tool: multiple_choice  options:

text.content:
["They're both 14, the same product", "They're different because the expressions are different", "The second product is bigger", "You can't tell without counting again"]
"What do you notice about the two products?"

rich_text.plain_text:
["They're both 14, the same product", "They're different because the expressions are different", "The second product is bigger", "You can't tell without counting again"]
"What do you notice about the two products?"

text.content: ✅ Student selected option A, observes product equivalence

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected option A, observes product equivalence

text.content: "Same product. Two different expressions, but they both equal 14. Hmm. You noticed something like that last time too."

rich_text.plain_text: "Same product. Two different expressions, but they both equal 14. Hmm. You noticed something like that last time too."

text.content: ❌ Student selected option B: believes different expressions must yield different products

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected option B: believes different expressions must yield different products

text.content: Product 14 highlights in row expression

rich_text.plain_text: Product 14 highlights in row expression

text.content: Product 14 highlights in column expression

rich_text.plain_text: Product 14 highlights in column expression

text.content: "The expressions are different, yes. But look at what they both equal. Check the number after the equals sign in each expression. What do you notice?"

rich_text.plain_text: "The expressions are different, yes. But look at what they both equal. Check the number after the equals sign in each expression. What do you notice?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected option C: believes 7 × 2 yields a larger product than 2 × 7

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected option C: believes 7 × 2 yields a larger product than 2 × 7

text.content: Product 14 highlights in row expression

rich_text.plain_text: Product 14 highlights in row expression

text.content: Product 14 highlights in column expression

rich_text.plain_text: Product 14 highlights in column expression

text.content: "Look at the products carefully. Remember that product is the result of a multiplication expression. What do the two expressions equal? Is one number bigger than the other?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Look at the products carefully. Remember that product is the result of a multiplication expression. What do the two expressions equal? Is one number bigger than the other?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected option D: believes the products cannot be compared without recounting

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected option D: believes the products cannot be compared without recounting

text.content: Product 14 highlights in row expression

rich_text.plain_text: Product 14 highlights in row expression

text.content: Product 14 highlights in column expression

rich_text.plain_text: Product 14 highlights in column expression

text.content: "You're working on it. We already have both products shown. Look at what each expression equals without counting again. What do you notice?"

rich_text.plain_text: "You're working on it. We already have both products shown. Look at what each expression equals without counting again. What do you notice?"

text.content: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

text.content: Product 14 highlights in row expression

rich_text.plain_text: Product 14 highlights in row expression

text.content: Product 14 highlights in column expression

rich_text.plain_text: Product 14 highlights in column expression

text.content: "Let me explain how to think about this. The row expression says 2 × 7 = 14. The column expression says 7 × 2 = 14. Both products are 14. The answer is "They're both 14, the same product." Even though the expressions look different, they give us the same total."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me explain how to think about this. The row expression says 2 × 7 = 14. The column expression says 7 × 2 = 14. Both products are 14. The answer is "They're both 14, the same product." Even though the expressions look different, they give us the same total."

text.content: "Last time, you spotted two expressions with the same product. And you just saw it again with a different array. But here's the big question: does that ALWAYS happen? And if it does, WHY? Let's find out."
